LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MSK HERITAGE'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND REASONABLE COSTS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND REASONABLE COSTS GRANTED IN PART. COUNSEL'S HOURLY RATES ARE REASONABLE, HOWEVER THE REQUESTED FEES ARE EXCESSIVE. THE COURT REDUCES PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL'S HOURS BY 100 HOURS EQUAL TO A REDUCTION OF $45,500, RESULTING IN A REASONABLE AWARD OF ATTORNEY FEES FOR REASONABLE AND NECESSARY WORK DONE IN THE AMOUNT OF $47,315. THE COURT REDUCES THE COSTS TO THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF $3,027.80, DISALLOWING $2,200 OF THE COSTS OF THE COMPUTER ASSISTED RESEARCH. TOTAL FEES AND COSTS AWARDED IS $50,342.80, AND THE COURT ADDS AN ADDITIONAL $10,500.00 TO THE FINAL AMOUNT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED, PLAINTIFF SHIFTS THE BURDEN AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. DEFENDANT SIGNED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND AGREED TO MOVE OUT. (PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBIT 2, PARAGRAPH 3.1.) DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE OUT AS AGREED (UMF6). NO AUTHORITY THAT THIS BREACH OF CONTRACT ACTION IS SUBJECT TO THE SFRO. NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT WAIVER, ESTOPPEL, OR ANY OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE RAISED IN PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION HAS MERIT OR WOULD PROVIDE A DEFENSE TO THE ISSUE OF POSSESSION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Msk Hertiage'S Ntc Of Motion For Award Of Reasonable Atty'S Fees And Reasonable Costs
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MSK Heritage's Motion for Award of Reasonable Attorney's Fees and Reasonable Costs. Appearances: Marte J. Bassi, of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP (415) 397-9006, appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Herod Hendrickson, Esq. of Lawrence M. Scaratelli (415) 398-1644 appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for award of reasonable attorney fees and reasonable costs granted in part. Counsel's hourly rates are reasonable, however the requested fees are excessive The court reduces plaintiffs' counsel's hours by 100 hours equal to a reduction of $45,500, resulting in a reasonable award of attorney fees for reasonable and necessary work done in the amount of $47,315. The court reduces the costs to the reasonable amount of $3,027.80, disallowing $2,200 of the costs of the computer assisted research. Total fees and costs awarded is $50,342.80, and the Court adds an additional $10,500.00 to the final amount. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Marte J. Bassi, Esq. of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP (415) 397-9006 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Jerod Hendrickson, Esq. Law Offices of Law Office of Lawrence M. Scancarelli (415) 398-1644 appearing for Defendant Robin Goldman and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, plaintiff shifts the burden and defendant fails to raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant signed the settlement agreement and agreed to move out. (Plaintiff's exhibit 2, paragraph 3.1.) Defendant did not move out as agreed (UMF6). No authority that this breach of contract action is subject to the SFRO. No evidence presented that waiver, estoppel, or any other affirmative defense raised in plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication has merit or would provide a defense to the issue of possession. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
